sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 2

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 2:1
items.html: (to the question) When the Partzuf rises for the sake of Gadlut as MAN [Mayin Nukvin] to its upper one, and attains there its fallen AHP [Awzen, Hotem, Peh — ear, nose, mouth] by the lowering of the Hey Tachtona [lower Hey] from the Eynaim [eyes], as is known — then the MAN of the degree below the ascending Partzuf rise together with it. For this is the rule: every AHP of a degree has the GE [Galgalta ve Eynaim] of the lower one of that degree incorporated in it. Because of this, in every raising of MAN, three degrees are necessarily included together: the upper one, rising with its own AHP to the upper-upper [its upper one's upper one], takes with it the GE of its lower one, which cleave to its AHP; thus two kinds of MAN are present in the upper-upper. However, the level that emerged upon the added MAN of the lower one's GE, the lower one cannot receive, since the lower one cannot receive any form in the upper-upper, for every degree must receive only from its own upper one. Therefore the upper one takes that entire level, in the secret of "the firstborn takes a double portion." Rather, afterwards, when the upper one descends to its place, the upper one itself couples upon the GE of the lower one that cleave to its AHP, and this time the lower one receives the form of its Ibur [conception], which is NRNHY [Nefesh, Ruach, Neshama, Haya, Yechida] de Nefesh. Thus the GE of the lower one have two Zivugim here: the first is its serving as MAN in the upper-upper together with its own upper one — this is called the first Zivug upon the MAN, or "first MAN," meaning in the third degree above them; hence in this first Zivug the MAN receive no form. And the second Zivug of the MAN means the Zivug made in its own upper one, as it must be according to the degree; hence in the second Zivug it receives the form of the NRNHY of its Ibur. Thus it has been explained that there is no MAN in the worlds that is not obliged to come twice in the discernment of MAN: first in the upper-upper, without form; and second in the upper one, where it takes form in Ibur. (Item 8)
